mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW X3 30e xDrive engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned X3 30e engine (B48 PHEV) typically costs £2,200–£4,500 supply only depending on generation. A reconditioned engine for a BMW X3 2.0‑litre typically falls between £1,200 and £2,800 for the engine alone. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,400. As a PHEV, the hybrid system components add complexity and cost. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your vehicle.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW X3 30e xDrive engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B48 is a robust and refined engine – part of BMW's B‑family, known for its reliability. Common issues include coolant leaks (expansion tank, water pump), PCV valve failure, and oil leaks. The plug‑in hybrid system adds complexity – HV battery degradation and charging system failures are the primary PHEV‑specific concerns. With regular maintenance and proper battery care, the X3 30e can be a reliable and efficient powertrain.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW X3 30e xDrive?

acceptedAnswer.text: Common issues include B48 coolant leaks (expansion tank, water pump, plastic fittings), PCV valve failure causing rough running and smoke, HV battery degradation (reduced electric range), and oil leaks from the valve cover and oil filter housing. Some owners have also reported electronic control units refusing to enter low‑power sleep mode, causing overnight battery drain.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing an X3 30e engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Given the X3's retained value (typically £20,000‑£45,000+), a £2,200–£4,500 reconditioned engine is a modest fraction of the vehicle's worth. Replacement is almost always financially viable. However, if the HV battery has also failed (costing £2,000‑£5,000+), the total repair cost may be substantial. Get a full diagnostic assessment before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my X3's engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The X3 30e uses the B48B20A (G01, 2022‑2024) or B48B20V (G45, 2025–present) 2.0‑litre turbo petrol engine combined with an electric motor. The engine code is stamped on the engine block and listed on your V5C. The 30e badge itself confirms the powertrain – it's the plug‑in hybrid offered in the X3 range.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair an X3 30e engine issue, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most issues – B48 coolant leaks, PCV valve failure, oil leaks – are repairable. HV battery degradation may be covered under warranty if the vehicle is still within the warranty period (typically 8 years/100,000 miles). However, if the engine suffers major internal failure, a full engine replacement is usually more cost‑effective than a rebuild.

replacementCosts.h2: BMW X3 30e xDrive Engine Replacement Costs

rows.bestFor: Insurance jobs; warranty claims; full provenance

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the X3 30e pricing. A reconditioned B48 PHEV engine for a BMW X3 typically falls between £1,200 and £2,800 for the engine alone. The PHEV system components (electric motor, battery, inverter) add complexity and cost to any replacement. Fitting costs typically add £600–£1,000.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an X3 30e engine typically takes 12‑16 hours at £75‑£110/hr (independent BMW specialist). PHEV system integration adds to the complexity.

cta.label: → Compare X3 30e xDrive engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: B48 Coolant Leaks — Expansion Tank & Water Pump
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss without visible external leaks, engine overheating, warning lights, low coolant level message.
problems.repairCost: £500–£1,500 [PLACEHOLDER] (expansion tank, water pump, or coolant lines replacement).
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (if caught early; ignored coolant loss can cause overheating and engine damage).

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if coolant loss or overheating occurs.

problems.recommendation: The B48 is a robust engine, but coolant leaks from the expansion tank, water pump, and plastic fittings are a common issue. The B48's water pump is driven by the serpentine belt and can fail. If the expansion tank or water pump develops a leak, the engine can overheat rapidly. A £500–£1,500 cooling system repair is far cheaper than the consequences of a warped head or blown head gasket. Regular inspection of coolant levels is essential.

problems.title: B48 PCV Valve Failure
problems.symptoms: Rough idle, engine misfire, blue/white smoke from exhaust, engine warning light, whistling noise from the engine.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (if caught early; ignored PCV failure can lead to oil consumption and catalytic converter damage).

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if symptoms develop.

problems.recommendation: The B48's PCV valve is a known weak point. A failing PCV valve can cause rough running, engine misfires, and blue/white smoke from the exhaust due to oil being drawn into the intake. If the PCV valve fails, the engine may run rough and consume oil. Early diagnosis and replacement are essential — ignoring it can lead to more expensive repairs.

problems.title: HV Battery Degradation (PHEV)
problems.symptoms: Reduced electric‑only range, longer charging times, hybrid system warnings on the dashboard, the car running only on petrol.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — battery replacement only (if ignored, the vehicle runs on petrol only, reducing fuel economy).

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if HV battery warnings appear or if electric range drops significantly.

problems.recommendation: The X3 30e's lithium‑ion battery is a significant component. Over time, battery degradation is inevitable — the range will drop, and charging may take longer. BMW batteries are generally well‑engineered, but if the battery fails outside of the warranty period (typically 6‑8 years), replacement is expensive. If you notice a significant drop in electric range or warning lights, have the battery health checked by a specialist. Some owners have reported electronic control units refusing to enter low‑power sleep mode, causing overnight battery drain.

problems.title: Oil Leaks — Oil Filter Housing & Valve Cover (B48)
problems.symptoms: Oil spots under the vehicle, burning oil smell, low oil level warning, visible oil leak from the top or front of the engine.
problems.repairCost: £300–£800 [PLACEHOLDER] (valve cover gasket or oil filter housing gasket replacement).
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (if caught early; ignored oil loss can lead to engine damage).

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if oil leak is detected.

problems.recommendation: The 2.0‑litre turbocharged engine in the xDrive30e is known for its performance, but it can develop turbocharger issues and oil leaks over time. Common problems include oil leaks, worn bearings, or damaged turbine blades. If the oil filter housing gasket fails, oil can leak onto the serpentine belt, causing it to slip or break — which can then be ingested into the engine through the front crankshaft seal, causing catastrophic failure. If you notice oil spots under your X3 30e or a burning oil smell, investigate immediately.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: BMW X3 30e xDrive — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Worth fixing — common and well‑understood repair
May be covered under warranty; if not, assess cost vs. vehicle value
Worth fixing — common and well‑understood repair
Full engine replacement often more cost‑effective

buyingChecks.title: Buying This BMW X3 30e xDrive? Check These First
buyIf: * Full BMW service history with correct hybrid system maintenance
* HV battery health check completed and documented
* Evidence of regular charging and battery care
* Warranty remaining — the X3 30e is a complex vehicle to repair out of warranty
avoidIf: * Any sign of HV battery warnings or reduced electric range
* No evidence of regular charging — the hybrid battery may have been neglected
* Any sign of coolant loss — B48 cooling system issues are common
* No evidence of regular oil changes — the B48 is intolerant of neglect

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The BMW X3 30e xDrive represents a compelling plug‑in hybrid option in the compact SUV segment — combining the robust B48 2.0‑litre turbo petrol engine with electric drive for 299 PS and 450 Nm. The new G45 generation offers a significantly improved 22.3 kWh battery, delivering up to 56 miles of electric‑only range — enough for most daily commutes without using petrol. However, PHEV complexity brings potential issues — battery degradation, charging system failures, and the usual B48 cooling system and oil leak concerns. Buy on service history above all else — a vehicle with documented battery health checks and regular maintenance is a safe bet; a neglected example can be expensive to repair.

cta.label: → Compare X3 30e xDrive engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW X3 30e xDrive (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: 1. Electric Range — Does the displayed range match expectations? (G01: ~33‑50 km; G45: up to 56 miles)
2. Service History — Full BMW history with hybrid system maintenance records
3. HV Battery Health — Has the battery been tested? What's its state of health?
4. Charging — Does the car charge correctly? (KLE module and charging port check)
5. Coolant Level — Any sign of coolant loss? (B48 cooling system common issue)
6. PCV System — Any rough idle, misfire, or smoke? (PCV valve common issue)
7. Oil Leaks — Check under the vehicle and around the valve cover and oil filter housing
8. MOT History — Check for emissions‑related failures or any advisory notes
